label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about which statement is true regarding Yves Gomes. Let me start by going through the article and each option carefully. First, the article says Yves Gomes is now a university student in Maryland but may have to leave. He's an illegal immigrant who grew up in the US. He was taken to India when he was 14 months old, along with his parents, who were deported over a year ago. He wants to stay and finish college, go to medical school, and become a doctor. He supports the DREAMAct because he believes without it, their dreams are lost. The DREAMAct is pending, and it's been 10 years since it was introduced but hasn't passed. Republicans might block it this year, and Gomes hopes it doesn't happen so he can finish school and become a citizen. Now let's look at the options: (A) He was born in India before his parents immigrated to America illegally. The article says he was born in the US, right? Wait, the article says he's an illegal immigrant who grew up in the US. Wait, the wording is a bit confusing. Wait, he's an illegal immigrant, but he grew up in the US.
